extra.reasoning: The property is a monotonicity claim about the input stiff_neck: toggling stiff_neck from false to true (all other inputs fixed) must never lower acuity. Scanning all seven rules (raise_slurred_speech, raise_leg_swelling, raise_abdominal_pain at priority 3; raise_vomiting, raise_palpitations, raise_confusion, raise_fever at priority 2) plus the default (acuity := routine), none of them reference stiff_neck in their condition. The acuity output is therefore a function solely of the other seven inputs (slurred_speech, leg_swelling, abdominal_pain, vomiting, palpitations, confusion, fever). Holding those fixed and flipping stiff_neck leaves every rule's firing status and the default behavior unchanged, so the computed acuity is identical before and after. Equal acuity satisfies "no lower than before" (equal is not strictly lower). Because this holds for every input assignment in the entire input space, the monotonicity property holds. No counterexample exists. File analyzed: /Users/sanjaybasu/clinical-formal-verification/experiments/runs/_judge_prompts/triage-d5-hold-113.txt
